text:
Market today: Risk-off vibes dominating. Stock market saw its first outflow since March, and a Wall Street vet is warning earnings excitement is getting dangerous. That's a direct headwind for high-multiple tech and chip names — exactly your book. Margin amplifies every down-day.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 15% and flagged SELL, one bad session away from testing your $336 stop. PLTR is right behind it at -14.75% with a SELL flag — two large losers on margin is a real risk. SPAL is down 17.7% already; it's a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily — every hour you hold a loser like this costs you extra.

Watch: 1) Whether SPY/QQQ break down on volume — your HOLDs (SMH, CRWD) follow fast. 2) SPCX near its $150 stop — brand-new, thinly traded IPO with no history; it can move violently.

Bottom line: You have three positions bleeding on margin right now — AVGO, PLTR, and SPAL — decide on exits before the market decides for you.
